terminal.c(3) Library Functions Manual terminal.c(3)

NAME

terminal.c - Pixel renderer for render-file: libvterm + FreeType2 software compositor.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct glyph_cache_entry_t
struct terminal_renderer_s

Functions


asciichat_error_t term_renderer_create (const term_renderer_config_t *cfg, terminal_renderer_t **out)
asciichat_error_t term_renderer_feed (terminal_renderer_t *r, const char *ansi_frame, size_t len)
const uint8_t * term_renderer_pixels (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_width_px (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_height_px (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_pitch (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_get_cols (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_get_rows (terminal_renderer_t *r)
void term_renderer_destroy (terminal_renderer_t *r)

Detailed Description

Pixel renderer for render-file: libvterm + FreeType2 software compositor.

Cross-platform implementation using:

  • libvterm: Terminal emulation without any display backend
  • FreeType2: Glyph rasterization (RGBA framebuffer) with uthash-based glyph cache
  • fontconfig: Font resolution (Linux/macOS)
  • uthash: Hash table for caching pre-rendered glyphs

No platform-specific code (#if guards) needed — this code compiles on all platforms.

Definition in file media/render/terminal.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t term_renderer_create (const term_renderer_config_t * cfg, terminal_renderer_t ** out)

Definition at line 195 of file media/render/terminal.c.

195                                                                                                      {
196   terminal_renderer_t *r = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*r), terminal_renderer_t *);
197   r->cols = cfg->cols;
198   r->rows = cfg->rows;
199   r->theme = cfg->theme;
200 
201   if (FT_Init_FreeType(&r->ft_lib)) {
202     SAFE_FREE(r);
203     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "FreeType init failed");
204   }
205 
206   if (cfg->font_data && cfg->font_data_size > 0) {
207     if (FT_New_Memory_Face(r->ft_lib, cfg->font_data, (FT_Long)cfg->font_data_size, 0, &r->ft_face)) {
208       FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
209       SAFE_FREE(r);
210       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "FreeType: cannot load bundled font");
211     }
212   } else {
213     if (FT_New_Face(r->ft_lib, cfg->font_spec, 0, &r->ft_face)) {
214       FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
215       SAFE_FREE(r);
216       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "FreeType: cannot load font '%s'", cfg->font_spec);
217     }
218   }
219 
220   // Detect if this is the matrix font (needs character mapping to Private Use Area)
221   r->is_matrix_font = (strstr(cfg->font_spec, "matrix") != NULL || strstr(cfg->font_spec, "Matrix") != NULL) ||
222                       (cfg->font_data != NULL && GET_OPTION(matrix_rain));
223   if (r->is_matrix_font) {
224     log_debug("term_renderer_create: Detected matrix font - will use Private Use Area character mapping");
225   }
226 
227   // Handle scalable vs bitmap fonts differently
228   // Bitmap fonts (like matrix) don't respond to FT_Set_Char_Size
229   // Instead, use FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes to request a specific pixel size
230   if (r->ft_face->num_fixed_sizes > 0) {
231     // Bitmap font: use FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes to select appropriate size (12px is reasonable for ASCII)
232     int err = FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes(r->ft_face, 12, 12);
233     log_debug("term_renderer_create: [BITMAP] FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes(12, 12) returned %d", err);
234 
235     // If that fails, try FT_Select_Size as fallback
236     if (err != 0) {
237       err = FT_Select_Size(r->ft_face, 0); // Use first available strike
238       log_debug("term_renderer_create: [BITMAP FALLBACK] FT_Select_Size(0) returned %d", err);
239     }
240   } else {
241     // Scalable font: use FT_Set_Char_Size
242     // FT_Set_Char_Size takes 1/64pt units and DPI — supports fractional point sizes.
243     // 96 DPI is the standard screen DPI used here; the 64 factor is the 26.6 fixed-point scale.
244     int err = FT_Set_Char_Size(r->ft_face, 0, (FT_F26Dot6)(cfg->font_size_pt * 64.0), 96, 96);
245     log_debug("term_renderer_create: [SCALABLE] FT_Set_Char_Size(size_pt=%.1f → %ld 1/64pt) returned %d",
246               cfg->font_size_pt, (long)(cfg->font_size_pt * 64.0), err);
247   }
248 
249   // For matrix fonts, try PUA glyph first (U+E900) since ASCII chars might not have bitmap data
250   // For other fonts, start with 'M'
251   int load_err;
252   if (r->is_matrix_font) {
253     log_debug("Matrix font detected: trying PUA glyph U+E900 first");
254     load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 0xE900, FT_LOAD_RENDER);
255     log_debug("FT_Load_Char(U+E900) returned %d", load_err);
256 
257     // If PUA glyph fails, try ASCII fallback
258     if (load_err != 0) {
259       log_debug("U+E900 failed, trying fallback character 'M'");
260       load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 'M', FT_LOAD_RENDER);
261       log_debug("FT_Load_Char('M') returned %d", load_err);
262     }
263   } else {
264     // For non-matrix fonts, standard approach
265     load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 'M', FT_LOAD_RENDER);
266     log_debug("DEBUG: FT_Load_Char('M', FT_LOAD_RENDER) returned %d", load_err);
267 
268     // For bitmap fonts (like matrix), FT_Load_Char may fail if the character doesn't exist.
269     // Try a fallback character if 'M' fails.
270     if (load_err != 0 && r->ft_face->num_fixed_sizes > 0) {
271       log_debug("FT_Load_Char('M') failed for bitmap font, trying fallback character 'A'");
272       load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 'A', FT_LOAD_RENDER);
273       log_debug("FT_Load_Char('A') returned %d", load_err);
274 
275       if (load_err != 0) {
276         // Last resort: try a PUA glyph for unknown bitmap fonts
277         log_debug("FT_Load_Char('A') also failed, trying PUA glyph U+E900");
278         load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 0xE900, FT_LOAD_RENDER);
279         log_debug("FT_Load_Char(U+E900) returned %d", load_err);
280       }
281     }
282   }
283 
284   // For monospace ASCII grid: use advance.x (proper character spacing) and rendered height
285   FT_Pos advance_x_26_6 = r->ft_face->glyph->advance.x;
286   log_debug("DEBUG: advance.x (26.6pt)=%ld", advance_x_26_6);
287 
288   // Calculate cell width from font metrics
289   r->cell_w = (int)(advance_x_26_6 >> 6);
290 
291   // Safeguard: for bitmap fonts, cell_w might be wrong if the font metrics are off
292   // Ensure we have a reasonable minimum width
293   if (r->cell_w <= 0) {
294     log_warn("term_renderer_create: cell_w is %d (invalid), using default 10px", r->cell_w);
295     r->cell_w = 10;
296   }
297 
298   // Only add spacing to fill entire frame if --stretch is specified
299   bool stretch_mode = GET_OPTION(stretch);
300   if (stretch_mode) {
301     r->cell_w++;
302     log_debug("ADVANCE_X: value=%ld (26.6pt) → cell_w=%d (advance + 1 for stretch), cols=%d → width_px=%d",
303               advance_x_26_6, r->cell_w, r->cols, r->cols * r->cell_w);
304   } else {
305     log_debug("ADVANCE_X: value=%ld (26.6pt) → cell_w=%d (preserve aspect ratio), cols=%d → width_px=%d",
306               advance_x_26_6, r->cell_w, r->cols, r->cols * r->cell_w);
307   }
308 
309   // For both bitmap and scalable fonts, use the actual glyph bitmap height
310   // This ensures text doesn't overflow cells. Using size->metrics.height (line spacing)
311   // instead of bitmap.rows causes cells to be too large and text to overflow.
312   r->cell_h = r->ft_face->glyph->bitmap.rows;
313   int raw_bitmap_top = r->ft_face->glyph->bitmap_top;
314   log_debug("DEBUG: cell_h=%d (from bitmap.rows), raw_bitmap_top=%d", r->cell_h, raw_bitmap_top);
315 
316   // Fallback: if cell_h is 0 (bitmap failed to load), use aspect ratio calculation
317   if (r->cell_h <= 0) {
318     log_warn("term_renderer_create: cell_h is 0 (FT_Load_Char failed or no bitmap), using aspect ratio fallback");
319     r->cell_h = r->cell_w * 2;
320     log_debug("FALLBACK: cell_h set to %d (2x width=%d)", r->cell_h, r->cell_w);
321   }
322 
323   // Apply aspect ratio correction unless --stretch is specified
324   // Terminal characters are typically 2:1 (height:width) to appear normal
325   // BUT: only correct if the loaded glyph height is reasonably close (±30%)
326   // This prevents huge jumps from small glyphs (e.g., 8px → 32px)
327   if (!stretch_mode && r->cell_h > 0) {
328     int corrected_h = r->cell_w * 2;
329     int min_acceptable = (corrected_h * 70) / 100;  // 70% of target
330     int max_acceptable = (corrected_h * 130) / 100; // 130% of target
331 
332     if (r->cell_h < min_acceptable || r->cell_h > max_acceptable) {
333       // Loaded height is far from expected, apply correction
334       log_debug("ASPECT_RATIO: Correcting cell_h from %d to %d (out of range [%d,%d], 2x width=%d)",
335                 r->cell_h, corrected_h, min_acceptable, max_acceptable, r->cell_w);
336       r->cell_h = corrected_h;
337     } else if (corrected_h != r->cell_h) {
338       // Loaded height is close to expected, fine-tune by small amount only
339       int delta = corrected_h - r->cell_h;
340       if (delta > 4 || delta < -4) {
341         // Only apply if difference is more than 4px
342         log_debug("ASPECT_RATIO: Fine-tuning cell_h from %d to %d (within acceptable range, 2x width=%d)",
343                   r->cell_h, corrected_h, r->cell_w);
344         r->cell_h = corrected_h;
345       } else {
346         log_debug("ASPECT_RATIO: Keeping cell_h=%d (close to target %d, delta=%d)", r->cell_h, corrected_h, delta);
347       }
348     }
349   }
350 
351   // Position baseline vertically within the cell.
352   // Baseline should be positioned at approximately 75% of cell height to allow proper
353   // glyph rendering with space both above and below. This prevents glyphs from being
354   // clipped at the cell boundaries.
355   //
356   // Baseline position: y_glyph = py + r->baseline - g->bitmap_top
357   // We want: 0 <= baseline - bitmap_top < cell_h (glyph fits within cell)
358   // Choose: baseline = (3 * cell_h) / 4 (positions at 75% height)
359   r->baseline = (3 * r->cell_h) / 4;
360   log_debug("DEBUG: baseline set to %d (75%% of cell_h=%d)", r->baseline, r->cell_h);
361 
362   r->width_px = r->cols * r->cell_w;
363   r->height_px = r->rows * r->cell_h;
364   log_debug("DEBUG: calculated dimensions: width_px=%d (cols=%d * cell_w=%d), height_px=%d (rows=%d * cell_h=%d)",
365             r->width_px, r->cols, r->cell_w, r->height_px, r->rows, r->cell_h);
366   // Pitch in bytes: each pixel is 4 bytes (RGBA), already aligned to 4-byte boundary
367   int base_pitch = r->width_px * 4;
368   r->pitch = base_pitch;
369   log_debug("PITCH_CALC: width_px=%d, base_pitch=%d, final_pitch=%d (RGBA, no padding needed)", r->width_px, base_pitch,
370             r->pitch);
371   log_debug("DEBUG: Allocating framebuffer: %d bytes total (%d pitch * %d height)",
372             (int)((size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px), r->pitch, r->height_px);
373   r->framebuffer = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px, uint8_t *);
374 
375   r->vt = vterm_new(r->rows, r->cols);
376   r->vts = vterm_obtain_screen(r->vt);
377 
378   // Enable UTF-8 encoding so vterm correctly parses multi-byte characters
379   vterm_set_utf8(r->vt, 1);
380 
381   // Set size BEFORE reset to ensure screen is allocated with correct dimensions
382   vterm_set_size(r->vt, r->rows, r->cols);
383   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_set_size called with rows=%d cols=%d", r->rows, r->cols);
384 
385   vterm_screen_set_callbacks(r->vts, &g_vterm_cbs, r);
386   // Reset MUST come before any input writing to initialize state correctly
387   vterm_screen_reset(r->vts, 1);
388 
389   // Verify the size was set correctly after reset
390   int vt_rows, vt_cols;
391   vterm_get_size(r->vt, &vt_rows, &vt_cols);
392   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_get_size returned rows=%d cols=%d (expected %d,%d)", vt_rows, vt_cols, r->rows, r->cols);
393 
394   // CRITICAL: Set size AGAIN after reset, as reset may have affected it
395   vterm_set_size(r->vt, r->rows, r->cols);
396   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_set_size called AGAIN after reset");
397 
398   // Initialize per-renderer glyph cache (empty, will populate on demand)
399   r->glyph_cache = NULL;
400 
401   *out = r;
402   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
403 }

    terminal_renderer_s::baseline, terminal_renderer_s::cell_h,

void
  term_renderer_destroy (terminal_renderer_t * r)


Definition at line 556 of file
    media/render/terminal.c.


556                                                    {
557   if (!r)
558     return;
559   vterm_free(r->vt);
560   FT_Done_Face(r->ft_face);
561   FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
562   SAFE_FREE(r->framebuffer);
563   glyph_cache_destroy(&r->glyph_cache);
564   SAFE_FREE(r);

asciichat_error_t term_renderer_feed
  (terminal_renderer_t * r, const char * ansi_frame, size_t len)


Definition at line 405 of file
    media/render/terminal.c.


405                                                                                                  {
406   // Clear framebuffer to ensure no leftover pixels from previous frames
407   uint8_t def_bg = (r->theme == TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT) ? 255 : 0;
408   memset(r->framebuffer, def_bg, (size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px);
409 
410   // Reset scrolling region and cursor position to prevent vertical shifts
411   static const char reset_sequence[] = "\033[2J\033[H\033[r";
412   vterm_input_write(r->vt, reset_sequence, sizeof(reset_sequence) - 1);
413 
414   // Count newlines to determine CRLF conversion size
415   int newline_count = 0;
416   for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
417     if (ansi_frame[i] == '\n')
418       newline_count++;
419   }
420 
421   // Convert LF-only line endings to CRLF (vterm expects CRLF)
422   // This fixes cursor positioning on alternate rows
423   char *fixed_frame = SAFE_MALLOC(len + newline_count, char *);
424   if (!fixed_frame) {
425     log_error("failed to allocate memory for CRLF conversion");
426     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "CRLF conversion allocation failed");
427   }
428 
429   size_t fixed_pos = 0;
430   for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
431     fixed_frame[fixed_pos++] = ansi_frame[i];
432     if (ansi_frame[i] == '\n' && (i == 0 || ansi_frame[i - 1] != '\r')) {
433       // Insert carriage return before LF if not already preceded by CR
434       fixed_frame[fixed_pos - 1] = '\r';
435       fixed_frame[fixed_pos++] = '\n';
436     }
437   }
438 
439   vterm_input_write(r->vt, fixed_frame, fixed_pos);
440   SAFE_FREE(fixed_frame);
441 
442   // Calculate centering offsets to position grid in center of canvas
443   int grid_width_px = r->cols * r->cell_w;
444   int grid_height_px = r->rows * r->cell_h;
445   int offset_x = (r->width_px - grid_width_px) / 2;
446   int offset_y = (r->height_px - grid_height_px) / 2;
447 
448   static int frame_count = 0;
449   static char prev_row_sigs[52][5] = {0}; // Store signatures of previous frame's rows
450   frame_count++;
451 
452   // Log if any row signature changed to detect vertical shifts
453   if (frame_count % 10 == 0) {
454     char curr_row_sigs[52][5];
455     for (int row = 0; row < r->rows && row < 52; row++) {
456       // Get first 4 chars as signature
457       for (int col = 0; col < 4 && col < r->cols; col++) {
458         VTermScreenCell cell;
459         vterm_screen_get_cell(r->vts, (VTermPos){row, col}, &cell);
460         curr_row_sigs[row][col] = (cell.chars[0] >= 32 && cell.chars[0] <= 126) ? cell.chars[0] : '.';
461       }
462       curr_row_sigs[row][4] = 0;
463     }
464     memcpy(prev_row_sigs, curr_row_sigs, sizeof(curr_row_sigs));
465   }
466 
467   int glyph_rendered_count = 0;
468   for (int row = 0; row < r->rows; row++) {
469     for (int col = 0; col < r->cols; col++) {
470       VTermScreenCell cell;
471       vterm_screen_get_cell(r->vts, (VTermPos){row, col}, &cell);
472 
473       uint8_t fr, fg, fb, br, bg, bb;
474       if (VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.fg)) {
475         fr = cell.fg.rgb.red;
476         fg = cell.fg.rgb.green;
477         fb = cell.fg.rgb.blue;
478       } else {
479         fr = fg = fb = 204;
480       }
481 
482       if (VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.bg)) {
483         br = cell.bg.rgb.red;
484         bg = cell.bg.rgb.green;
485         bb = cell.bg.rgb.blue;
486       } else {
487         br = bg = bb = def_bg;
488       }
489 
490       int px = offset_x + col * r->cell_w, py = offset_y + row * r->cell_h;
491       for (int dy = 0; dy < r->cell_h; dy++) {
492         int y = py + dy;
493         if (y < 0 || y >= r->height_px)
494           continue;
495         uint8_t *line = r->framebuffer + y * r->pitch + px * 4;
496         for (int dx = 0; dx < r->cell_w; dx++) {
497           int x = px + dx;
498           if (x < 0 || x >= r->width_px)
499             continue;
500           line[dx * 4] = br;
501           line[dx * 4 + 1] = bg;
502           line[dx * 4 + 2] = bb;
503           line[dx * 4 + 3] = 255;
504         }
505       }
506 
507       if (cell.chars[0] && cell.chars[0] != ' ') {
508         // For matrix font, map ASCII characters to Private Use Area glyphs (U+E900-U+E91A)
509         uint32_t char_to_render = r->is_matrix_font ? matrix_char_map(cell.chars[0]) : cell.chars[0];
510 
511         // Use per-renderer glyph cache to avoid rasterizing every glyph every frame
512         FT_Bitmap *cached_bitmap = glyph_cache_get(r, r->ft_face, char_to_render);
513         if (cached_bitmap && cached_bitmap->width > 0 && cached_bitmap->rows > 0) {
514           // Find cache entry to get positioning offsets
515           glyph_cache_entry_t *cache_entry = NULL;
516           HASH_FIND_INT(r->glyph_cache, &char_to_render, cache_entry);
517           if (cache_entry) {
518             blit_glyph(r, cached_bitmap, px + cache_entry->bitmap_left, py + r->baseline - cache_entry->bitmap_top,
519                         fr, fg, fb, br, bg, bb);
520             glyph_rendered_count++;
521           }
522         }
523       }
524     }
525   }
526   log_info("term_renderer_feed: Rendered %d glyphs out of %d cells", glyph_rendered_count, r->rows * r->cols);
527 
528   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
529 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    terminal_renderer_s::baseline,
    glyph_cache_entry_t::bitmap_left,
    glyph_cache_entry_t::bitmap_top, terminal_renderer_s::cell_h,
    terminal_renderer_s::cell_w, terminal_renderer_s::cols,
    ERROR_MEMORY, terminal_renderer_s::framebuffer,
    terminal_renderer_s::ft_face,
    terminal_renderer_s::glyph_cache,
    terminal_renderer_s::height_px,
    terminal_renderer_s::is_matrix_font, log_error,
    log_info, terminal_renderer_s::pitch,
    terminal_renderer_s::rows, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SET_ERRNO, TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT,
    terminal_renderer_s::theme, terminal_renderer_s::vt,
    terminal_renderer_s::vts, and
  terminal_renderer_s::width_px.

    Version 0.11.2
    ascii-chat
